name: Alzamend Neuro, Inc.
business: Alzamend Neuro is a clinical-stage biopharmaceutical company developing novel product candidates for Alzheimer's disease, bipolar disorder, major depressive disorder, and post-traumatic stress disorder. The company's pipeline includes AL001, an ionic cocrystal of lithium, and ALZN002, a cell-based therapeutic vaccine, both licensed from the University of South Florida Research Foundation.
sellsTo: Patients with Alzheimer's disease, bipolar disorder, major depressive disorder, and post-traumatic stress disorder
namedSuppliers: University of South Florida Research Foundation, Inc.

exposures.sentence: We are a clinical-stage biopharmaceutical company focused on developing novel products for the treatment of Alzheimer s disease ( Alzheimer s ), bipolar disorder ( BD ), major depressive disorder ( MDD ) and post-traumatic stress disorder ( PTSD ).

catalysts.event: Phase I clinical trial of AL001 completed
catalysts.sentence: The Phase I study was completed in March 2022 with the following results: AL001 was shown to be safe and well-tolerated in healthy adult subjects;

catalysts.event: Phase IIA MAD clinical trial completed
catalysts.sentence: We completed the Phase IIA clinical trial in March 2023, announced positive topline data in June 2023 and announced full data sets in October 2024.

catalysts.event: Topline data from Phase IIA announced
catalysts.sentence: announced positive topline data in June 2023

catalysts.event: Full data set from Phase IIA announced
catalysts.sentence: announced full data sets in October 2024.

catalysts.event: Partnership with Massachusetts General Hospital for five Phase II imaging trials announced
catalysts.sentence: In August 2024, we announced a partnership with Massachusetts General Hospital ( MGH ) and Harvard Medical School to conduct five Phase II imaging clinical trials.

catalysts.event: First patient dosed in healthy subject Phase II imaging trial
catalysts.sentence: In May 2025, we announced the initiation, enrollment and dosing of the first patient for the healthy human patients.

catalysts.event: Expect to present topline data from healthy subject study
catalysts.sentence: We expect to present topline data from this study by the end of 2025.
On September 28, 2022, we submitted an IND to the FDA for ALZN002 and received a study may proceed letter on October 31, 2022.

catalysts.event: Initiation of Phase I/IIA clinical trial for ALZN002
catalysts.sentence: On April 3, 2023, we announced the initiation of a Phase I/IIA clinical trial for ALZN002 to treat mild to moderate dementia of the Alzheimer s type.

catalysts.event: Termination of CRO Biorasi for ALZN002 trial
catalysts.sentence: In February 2024, we received notice from Biorasi, our CRO, that Biorasi was terminating our contract with it.
We do not expect to resume this trial until mid-2026.

tamClaims.claim: Alzheimer's currently affects roughly 7.2 million Americans; expected to grow to 13 million by 2050
tamClaims.sentence: Alzheimer s is also the seventh leading cause of death (in 2021 and 2022) in the United States ... currently affects roughly 7.2 million Americans; that number is expected to grow to 13 million individuals by 2050.

tamClaims.claim: Estimated healthcare costs for Alzheimer's in 2025 will be $384 billion, rising to $1 trillion per year by 2050
tamClaims.sentence: In 2025, the estimated healthcare costs for treating individuals with Alzheimer s in the U.S. will be $384 billion, including $246 billion in Medicare and Medicaid payments. These costs could rise to as high as $1 trillion per year by 2050 if no permanent treatment or cure for Alzheimer s is found, according to the Alzheimer s Association.

tamClaims.claim: More than 48.2 million Americans afflicted with Alzheimer's, BD, MDD or PTSD
tamClaims.sentence: According to the National Institute of Health ( NIH ), there are more than 48.2 million Americans afflicted with Alzheimer s, BD, MDD or PTSD.
